#Get Parameter from controldb
import json
db_port = 1433
linked_service_name = "LS_SQL_ELTFrameworkDB"
db_notebookname= "SharePoint_Gold"
linkedServiceProperties = json.loads(mssparkutils.credentials.getPropertiesAll(linked_service_name))
sql_server_name = linkedServiceProperties.get('Endpoint')
db_name = linkedServiceProperties.get('Database')
#print(linkedServiceProperties)
access_token = mssparkutils.credentials.getConnectionStringOrCreds(linked_service_name)

db_query ="SELECT A.*,B.NotebookParameterId,B.ParameterType,B.Value  FROM [CTL].[CFG_NOTEBOOK] A LEFT JOIN [CTL].[CFG_NOTEBOOK_PARAMETER] B ON A.NotebookId = B.NotebookId"

df = spark.read.format("jdbc") \
    .option("url", "jdbc:sqlserver://%s:%s" % (sql_server_name, db_port)) \
    .option("query", db_query) \
    .option("accessToken", access_token) \
    .option("encrypt", "true") \
    .option("databaseName", db_name) \
    .option("hostNameInCertificate", "*.database.windows.net") \
    .load()

df2 = df.where(df.NotebookPath==db_notebookname)

#Source DataLake storage details
source_storage = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='source_storage').collect()[0]["Value"] #Storage name
source_container = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='source_container').collect()[0]["Value"] #Storage container detail
source_path = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='source_path').collect()[0]["Value"] #internal folder location in container "demodata/demo_aemo"
#source_file = '' #CUSTOMER__full_32a72bd2-bd94-467f-9030-6b9f20dad676_20240204_100201

#Destination DataLake storage details
destination_storage = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='destination_storage').collect()[0]["Value"] #Storage name
destination_container = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='destination_container').collect()[0]["Value"] #raw storage container details
destination_path = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='destination_path').collect()[0]["Value"] #raw delta file patch

#Get Current Date
Gold_LoadDate=spark.sql("select current_timestamp()").collect()[0][0]

#Synapse Dedicated Server detials
SynapseDedicatedServer = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='destination_storage').collect()[0]['SynapseDedicatedServer'] 
TempFolder = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='destination_storage').collect()[0]['TempFolder']
SynapseDedicatedDatabase = df2.where(df2.ParameterType=='destination_storage').collect()[0]['SynapseDedicatedDatabase']

# Display values
print(source_storage)
print(source_container)
print(source_path)
print(destination_storage)
print(destination_container)
print(destination_path)
print(SynapseDedicatedServer)
print(TempFolder)
print(SynapseDedicatedDatabase)

#Create Bronze Table

spark.sql(f"""CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS {db_notebookname};""")
spark.sql(f"""
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}
(
    ID INT,
    SPM_NameHashId STRING,
    StationName STRING,
    Capacity DOUBLE,
    StartDate DATE,
    EndDate DATE,
    MLF DOUBLE,
    AuxiliaryLoss DOUBLE,
    NumberOfUnits DOUBLE, 
    Duration INT,
    FileName STRING,
    Lacima_loaddate TIMESTAMP
)
USING delta
LOCATION 'abfss://{destination_container}@{destination_storage}.dfs.core.windows.net/{destination_path}/{db_tablename}'

# Create a temporary view
df_gold = spark.sql(f"""
                        SELECT ID, 
                        SPM_NameHashId,  
                        StationName, 
                        Capacity, 
                        StartDate, 
                        EndDate, 
                        MLF, 
                        AuxiliaryLoss,
                        NumberOfUnits,
                        Duration,
                        FileName,
                        Lacima_loaddate 
                        FROM {source_dbname}.{source_tablename}

# Add Hash columns
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, concat, lit, to_timestamp, to_date, when
from pyspark.sql.functions import sha2, concat_ws, regexp_replace


#df_gold = df_gold.withColumn("SPM_NameHashId", sha2(concat_ws("||", "ID"), 256))

# Replace NULL values in usage
df_gold = df_gold.na.fill({"Capacity":0})
df_gold = df_gold.na.fill({"AuxiliaryLoss":0})
df_gold = df_gold.na.fill({"MLF":0})
df_gold = df_gold.withColumn("StartDate",when(col("StartDate").isNull(), to_date(lit("9999-12-31"), 'yyyy-MM-dd')).otherwise(col("StartDate")))
df_gold = df_gold.withColumn("EndDate",when(col("EndDate").isNull(), to_date(lit("9999-12-31"), 'yyyy-MM-dd')).otherwise(col("EndDate")))

#display(df_gold)

# Create a temporary view of the dataframe
df_gold.createOrReplaceTempView(f"""Gold_temp_{db_tablename}""")

# Use insert operation to update the target table
spark.sql(f"""
            INSERT INTO {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}
            SELECT      ID, 
                        SPM_NameHashId,  
                        StationName, 
                        Capacity, 
                        StartDate, 
                        EndDate, 
                        MLF, 
                        AuxiliaryLoss,
                        NumberOfUnits,
                        Duration, 
                        FileName,
                        Lacima_loaddate
            FROM Gold_temp_{db_tablename}
        """)

spark.sql(f"""
            DROP VIEW IF EXISTS Gold_temp_{db_tablename}

# Duplication count
Silver_duplicate_count= spark.sql(f"""
                                        SELECT sum(dCount)
                                        FROM (
                                                SELECT COUNT(SPM_NameHashId) AS dCount FROM {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}
                                                GROUP by SPM_NameHashId
                                                HAVING COUNT(SPM_NameHashId)>1 ) as subtb
                                    
                            """).collect()[0][0]
assert Silver_duplicate_count == None
print(Silver_duplicate_count)

# Write using AAD Auth to internal table
# Add required imports
import com.microsoft.spark.sqlanalytics
from com.microsoft.spark.sqlanalytics.Constants import Constants
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, concat, lit, to_timestamp,current_timestamp,date_format, to_date, when


#order columns for Azure Synapse insert should match synapse table creation order.
df_gold=df_gold.select("ID", "SPM_NameHashId", "StationName", "Capacity", "StartDate", "EndDate", "MLF", "AuxiliaryLoss", "NumberOfUnits", "Duration","FileName", "Lacima_loaddate")

# Configure and submit the request to write to Synapse Dedicated SQL Pool
# Sample below is using AAD-based authentication approach; See further examples to leverage SQL Basic auth.
(df_gold.write
 # If `Constants.SERVER` is not provided, the `<database_name>` from the three-part table name argument
 # to `synapsesql` method is used to infer the Synapse Dedicated SQL End Point.
 .option(Constants.SERVER, f"""{SynapseDedicatedServer}""")
 # Like-wise, if `Constants.TEMP_FOLDER` is not provided, the connector will use the runtime staging directory config (see section on Configuration Options for details).
 .option(Constants.TEMP_FOLDER, (f"""abfss://temp@{TempFolder}/{db_tablename}"""))
 # Choose a save mode that is apt for your use case.
 # Options for save modes are "error" or "errorifexists" (default), "overwrite", "append", "ignore".
 # refer to https://spark.apache.org/docs/latest/sql-data-sources-load-save-functions.html#save-modes
 .mode("overwrite")
 # Required parameter - Three-part table name to which data will be written
 .synapsesql(f"""{SynapseDedicatedDatabase}.dbo.{db_tablename}"""))

#Row count parameters
Gold_TotalRowCount_Post=spark.sql(f""" SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}""").collect()[0][0]

from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType, IntegerType, BooleanType,DateType, DoubleType,TimestampType

log_data = [(db_notebookname,db_tablename,Gold_LoadDate,Gold_TotalRows,0,0,Gold_TotalRows,Gold_TotalRows)]
print(log_data)
log_columns = ['PIPELINE_NAME', 'TABLE_NAME', 'INGEST_DATETIME', 'INSERT','UPDATE','DELETE','ACTIVE_ROWS','TOTAL_ROWS']
df_log = spark.createDataFrame(log_data, log_columns)
#display(df_test)

#Write to controldb
import json
db_port = 1433
linked_service_name = "LS_SQL_ELTFrameworkDB"
linkedServiceProperties = json.loads(mssparkutils.credentials.getPropertiesAll(linked_service_name))
sql_server_name = linkedServiceProperties.get('Endpoint')
db_name = linkedServiceProperties.get('Database')
access_token = mssparkutils.credentials.getConnectionStringOrCreds(linked_service_name)
write_table_name = "[CTL].[CFG_INGESTION_LOGS]"

try:
    df_log.write.format("jdbc") \
        .option("url", "jdbc:sqlserver://%s:%s" % (sql_server_name, db_port)) \
        .option("dbtable", write_table_name) \
        .option("accessToken", access_token) \
        .option("encrypt", "true") \
        .option("databaseName", db_name) \
        .option("hostNameInCertificate", "*.database.windows.net") \
        .mode("append") \
        .save()
except ValueError as error :
    print("MSSQL Connector write failed", error)

print("MSSQL Connector write(append) succeeded  ")

#Create Bronze Table

spark.sql(f"""CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS {db_notebookname};""")
spark.sql(f"""
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}
(
    ID INT,
    SPM_CategoryHashId STRING,
    Category1 STRING,
    Category2 STRING,
    Category3 STRING,
    Category4 STRING,
    Category5 STRING,
    Category6 STRING,
    FileName STRING,
    Lacima_loaddate TIMESTAMP
)
USING delta
LOCATION 'abfss://{destination_container}@{destination_storage}.dfs.core.windows.net/{destination_path}/{db_tablename}'

# Create a temporary view
df_gold = spark.sql(f"""
                        SELECT  ID, 
                                SPM_CategoryHashId, 
                                Category1, 
                                Category2, 
                                Category3, 
                                Category4, 
                                Category5, 
                                Category6, 
                                FileName,
                                Lacima_loaddate
                        FROM {source_dbname}.{source_tablename}

# Create a temporary view of the dataframe
df_gold.createOrReplaceTempView(f"""Gold_temp_{db_tablename}""")

# Use insert operation to update the target table
spark.sql(f"""
            INSERT INTO {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}
            SELECT      ID,
                        SPM_CategoryHashId, 
                        Category1, 
                        Category2, 
                        Category3, 
                        Category4, 
                        Category5, 
                        Category6, 
                        FileName,
                        Lacima_loaddate
            FROM Gold_temp_{db_tablename}
        """)

spark.sql(f"""
            DROP VIEW IF EXISTS Gold_temp_{db_tablename}

# Duplication count
Silver_duplicate_count= spark.sql(f"""
                                        SELECT sum(dCount)
                                        FROM (
                                                SELECT COUNT(SPM_CategoryHashId) AS dCount FROM {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}
                                                GROUP by SPM_CategoryHashId
                                                HAVING COUNT(SPM_CategoryHashId)>1 ) as subtb
                                    
                            """).collect()[0][0]
assert Silver_duplicate_count == None
print(Silver_duplicate_count)

# Write using AAD Auth to internal table
# Add required imports
import com.microsoft.spark.sqlanalytics
from com.microsoft.spark.sqlanalytics.Constants import Constants
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, concat, lit, to_timestamp,current_timestamp,date_format, to_date, when


#order columns for Azure Synapse insert should match synapse table creation order.
df_gold=df_gold.select("ID", "SPM_CategoryHashId", "Category1", "Category2", "Category3", "Category4", "Category5", "Category6", "FileName", "Lacima_loaddate")

# Configure and submit the request to write to Synapse Dedicated SQL Pool
# Sample below is using AAD-based authentication approach; See further examples to leverage SQL Basic auth.
(df_gold.write
 # If `Constants.SERVER` is not provided, the `<database_name>` from the three-part table name argument
 # to `synapsesql` method is used to infer the Synapse Dedicated SQL End Point.
 .option(Constants.SERVER, f"""{SynapseDedicatedServer}""")
 # Like-wise, if `Constants.TEMP_FOLDER` is not provided, the connector will use the runtime staging directory config (see section on Configuration Options for details).
 .option(Constants.TEMP_FOLDER, (f"""abfss://temp@{TempFolder}/{db_tablename}"""))
 # Choose a save mode that is apt for your use case.
 # Options for save modes are "error" or "errorifexists" (default), "overwrite", "append", "ignore".
 # refer to https://spark.apache.org/docs/latest/sql-data-sources-load-save-functions.html#save-modes
 .mode("overwrite")
 # Required parameter - Three-part table name to which data will be written
 .synapsesql(f"""{SynapseDedicatedDatabase}.dbo.{db_tablename}"""))

from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType, IntegerType, BooleanType,DateType, DoubleType,TimestampType

log_data = [(db_notebookname,db_tablename,Gold_LoadDate,Gold_TotalRows,0,0,Gold_TotalRows,Gold_TotalRows)]
print(log_data)
log_columns = ['PIPELINE_NAME', 'TABLE_NAME', 'INGEST_DATETIME', 'INSERT','UPDATE','DELETE','ACTIVE_ROWS','TOTAL_ROWS']
df_log = spark.createDataFrame(log_data, log_columns)
#display(df_test)

#Write to controldb
import json
db_port = 1433
linked_service_name = "LS_SQL_ELTFrameworkDB"
linkedServiceProperties = json.loads(mssparkutils.credentials.getPropertiesAll(linked_service_name))
sql_server_name = linkedServiceProperties.get('Endpoint')
db_name = linkedServiceProperties.get('Database')
access_token = mssparkutils.credentials.getConnectionStringOrCreds(linked_service_name)
write_table_name = "[CTL].[CFG_INGESTION_LOGS]"

try:
    df_log.write.format("jdbc") \
        .option("url", "jdbc:sqlserver://%s:%s" % (sql_server_name, db_port)) \
        .option("dbtable", write_table_name) \
        .option("accessToken", access_token) \
        .option("encrypt", "true") \
        .option("databaseName", db_name) \
        .option("hostNameInCertificate", "*.database.windows.net") \
        .mode("append") \
        .save()
except ValueError as error :
    print("MSSQL Connector write failed", error)

print("MSSQL Connector write(append) succeeded  ")

spark.sql(f"""CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS {db_notebookname};""")
spark.sql(f"""
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}
(
    SPM_Name_ID INT,
    SPM_NameHashId STRING,
    SPM_Category_ID INT,
    SPM_CategoryHashId STRING,
    PeriodsInQuarter INT,
    CalendarYear INT,
    FinancialYear INT,
    Quarter INT,
    StartDate DATE,
    DateHashId STRING,
    EndDate DATE,
    OperatingDays INT,
    SPM_LacimaRiskFactor_ID INT,
    SPM_LacimaRiskFactorHashId STRING,
    TotalLevelisedCostRecovery DOUBLE,
    RiskFactorNames STRING, 
    FileName STRING,
    LoadDate TIMESTAMP,
    Lacima_loaddate TIMESTAMP
)
USING delta
LOCATION 'abfss://{destination_container}@{destination_storage}.dfs.core.windows.net/{destination_path}/{db_tablename}'

# Create a temporary view

                        SELECT  rfc.SPM_Name_ID,
                                rfc.SPM_NameHashId, 
                                rfc.SPM_Category_ID,
                                rfc.SPM_CategoryHashId, 
                                rfc.PeriodsInQuarter, 
                                rfc.CalendarYear, 
                                rfc.FinancialYear, 
                                rfc.Quarter,

                                rfc.DateHashId, 
                                rfc.EndDate, 
                                rfc.OperatingDays, 
                                rfc.SPM_LacimaRiskFactor_ID,

                                lmf.RiskFactorNames,
                                rfc.FileName,
                                "{Gold_LoadDate}" AS LoadDate,
                                rfc.Lacima_loaddate
                                FROM {source_dbname}.{source_tablename} rfc
                                Left join {source_dbname}.spm_lacimariskfactor lmf on lmf.SPM_LacimaRiskFactorHashId = rfc.SPM_LacimaRiskFactorHashId

                        SELECT      SPM_Name_ID,
                                    SPM_NameHashId,
                                    SPM_Category_ID, 
                                    SPM_CategoryHashId,
                                    PeriodsInQuarter, 
                                    CalendarYear, 
                                    FinancialYear, 
                                    Quarter, 
                                    StartDate,
                                    DateHashId, 
                                    EndDate, 
                                    OperatingDays, 
                                    SPM_LacimaRiskFactor_ID,
                                    SPM_LacimaRiskFactorHashId, 
                                    TotalLevelisedCostRecovery,

                                    FileName,

                                    Lacima_loaddate
                        FROM {db_notebookname}.{db_tablename}
                    """)

df_gold=df_gold.select("SPM_Name_ID", "SPM_NameHashId", "SPM_Category_ID", "SPM_CategoryHashId", "PeriodsInQuarter", "CalendarYear", "FinancialYear", "Quarter", "StartDate", "DateHashId", "EndDate", "OperatingDays", "SPM_LacimaRiskFactor_ID", "SPM_LacimaRiskFactorHashId", "TotalLevelisedCostRecovery", "RiskFactorNames", "FileName", "LoadDate", "Lacima_loaddate")

(df_gold.write
 # If `Constants.SERVER` is not provided, the `<database_name>` from the three-part table name argument

 .option(Constants.SERVER, f"""{SynapseDedicatedServer}""")
 # Like-wise, if `Constants.TEMP_FOLDER` is not provided, the connector will use the runtime staging directory config (see section on Configuration Options for details).
 .option(Constants.TEMP_FOLDER, (f"""abfss://temp@{TempFolder}/{db_tablename}"""))

 .mode("overwrite")
 # Required parameter - Three-part table name to which data will be written
 .synapsesql(f"""{SynapseDedicatedDatabase}.dbo.{db_tablename}"""))

        .option("url", "jdbc:sqlserver://%s:%s" % (sql_server_name, db_port)) \
        .option("dbtable", write_table_name) \
        .option("accessToken", access_token) \
        .option("encrypt", "true") \
        .option("databaseName", db_name) \
        .option("hostNameInCertificate", "*.database.windows.net") \

        .save()
